title: Review and setlist: Dropkick Murphys fires up crowd, flames ICE at St. Paddy’s Day kickoff show
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Dropkick Murphys performed at MGM Music Hall on March 13, celebrating their 30th anniversary and kicking off St. Patrick's Day events.
The band included political commentary in their set, performing 'Citizen I.C.E.' against U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement policies.
Heather Fahey joined for 'The Dirty Glass'; show ended with hits like 'I’m Shipping Up to Boston' and 'The Big Man.'

title: He’s running Boston to support people like him with Type 1 diabetes
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Collin Bennett, 29, from New Braintree, Mass., is running the Boston Marathon for Team Beth Israel Lahey Health.
Bennett, diagnosed with Type 1 Diabetes at age 11, credits Joslin Diabetes Center for providing hope and guidance.
His marathon run supports Joslin Diabetes Center and aims to inspire those living with Type 1 Diabetes.

title: Springfield native Ruth E. Carter keeps making Oscars history
author: Lauren Daley
bullets: Ruth E. Carter becomes the most-nominated Black woman in Oscar history with her fifth nomination for 'Sinners'.
Carter was the first Black person to win an Oscar for Best Costume Design in 2019 for 'Black Panther'.
The Springfield native is designing her own gown for the Oscars, citing personal style and size considerations.

title: Brookline schools would see more than 200 layoffs if override fails, officials say
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Brookline schools face over 200 layoffs if property-tax override measure is rejected by voters.
The district needs $18.5 million over three years to maintain current programs and staffing levels.
Failure to secure funding may lead to class size increases and cuts in music and language courses.

title: What to know about the PETS Act aimed at protecting dogs and cats in Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: The PETS Act aims to ban the sale of dogs and cats in Massachusetts pet shops to curb puppy mill sales.
The bill promotes partnerships between pet stores, animal shelters, and rescue organizations for adoption processes.
Legislation seeks to address housing and insurance barriers, making pet ownership more accessible in Massachusetts.

title: As snow melts, drought still a big issue for Mass.
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: State officials urge water conservation as drought intensifies across Massachusetts despite February's heavy snowfall.
The Central and Northeast regions of Massachusetts are classified as Level 3 - Critical Drought.
Massachusetts Water Resources Authority's system remains undisturbed, though other areas face significant water shortages.

title: Why some women are hiding their boyfriends online
author: Meredith Goldstein
bullets: Chanté Joseph's essay on women hiding boyfriends online examines the reevaluation of traditional heterosexual relationships.
The essay questions societal norms about women centering male partners in their lives and discusses independence within relationships.
Joseph's work, which went viral, inspired discussion and art, including a song by Budapest band The Anahit.

title: He’s running the Boston Marathon for a camp ‘where illness takes a back seat to fun’
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Griffin O'Neill, 27, is running the Boston Marathon to support The Hole in the Wall Gang Camp.
The camp provides children with serious medical conditions a fun and medically safe environment free of charge.
O'Neill runs in honor of his fiancée's sister, a former camper who is now a counselor and cancer survivor.

title: Eagles reportedly ‘will not trade A.J. Brown at this time’ amid Patriots’ continued interest
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Eagles decline to trade A.J. Brown to the Patriots despite ongoing interest.
Trade talks could resume closer to June 1, when salary cap relief benefits the Eagles.
Patriots signed WR Romeo Doubs to a four-year deal, impacting their need for Brown.

title: Jayson Tatum set to return vs. Wizards after injury management off-day
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Jayson Tatum returns to play against the Wizards after missing a game for Achilles injury management.
Baylor Scheierman and Derrick White are probable; Nikola Vucevic remains out for the Celtics' game vs. Wizards.
Tatum played 27 minutes per game since his season debut, focusing on gradually increasing confidence in his right leg.
